claim: For all integers n >= 1, the number of divisors of n is odd if and only if n is a perfect square.
predicate: all((tau(n) % 2 == 1) == (round(n ** 0.5) ** 2 == n) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a perfect square, then sigma(n) is odd.
predicate: all((round(n ** 0.5) ** 2 != n) or (sigma(n) % 2 == 1) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if tau(n) is odd, then n is not divisible by any prime p to an odd power.
predicate: all((tau(n) % 2 != 1) or all(vp(n, p) % 2 == 0 for p in range(2, n+1) if is_prime(p)) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a perfect square, then phi(n) is even.

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is not a perfect square, then tau(n) is even.

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the sum of the divisors of n is odd if and only if n is a square or twice a square.
For all integers n >= 1, if n is a perfect square, then for every prime p dividing n, vp(n, p) is even.

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a perfect square, then n can be written as the product of an integer and a perfect square with no common factors.
predicate: all((round(n ** 0.5) ** 2 != n) or any(gcd(k, n // k) == 1 and round(k ** 0.5) ** 2 == k for k in range(1, n+1) if n % k == 0) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is not a perfect square, then the number of pairs (d, n/d) where d is a divisor of n is even.
predicate: all((round(n ** 0.5) ** 2 == n) or (sum(1 for d in range(1, n+1) if n % d == 0 and d < n / d) % 2 == 0) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, the number of unordered pairs of distinct divisors of n whose product is n is tau(n)/2 if n is not a perfect square, and (tau(n)-1)/2 if n is a perfect square.

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if sigma(n) is odd, then n is a perfect square or twice a perfect square.
